name: Remote: SMTP Mail Sender
notes: This rule triggers when a local host is sending a large number of SMTP flows from the same source to the Internet, in one interval. This could indicate a mass mailing, worm, or spam relay is present.
bb_references: BB:Policy Violation: Mail Policy Violation: Outbound Mail Sender
BB:Policy Violation: Mail Policy Violation: Outbound Mail Sender match at least 10 times with the same Source IP in 1 hour(s)
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local
when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
when a flow matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers
when BB:Policy Violation: Mail Policy Violation: Outbound Mail Sender match at least 10 times with the same Source IP in 1 hour(s)
thresholds: when BB:Policy Violation: Mail Policy Violation: Outbound Mail Sender match at least 10 times with the same Source IP in 1 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Remote: SMTP Mail Sender on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Local to Local
AND when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
AND NOT when a flow matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers
AND when BB:Policy Violation: Mail Policy Violation: Outbound Mail Sender match at least 10 times with the same Source IP in 1 hour(s)
RED_list: Event description: Detects a local host sending a large number of SMTP flows from the same source to the Internet, in one interval.  This may indicate a mass mailing, worm, or spam relay is present.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
